transcript.final_response:
Done. The three lists have been merged, deduplicated, and alphabetically sorted into `/data/combined.txt`.

**Result:**
```
alice@example.com
bob@example.com
charlie@example.com
dave@example.com
eve@example.com
frank@example.com
```

**Total unique emails: 6**

Notes on processing:
- Combined all three files
- Trimmed trailing whitespace and removed any blank lines
- Deduplicated removed the repeats (`bob`, `alice`, `eve` each appeared twice across files)
- Sorted alphabetically with `sort -u`
tool_calls: ls -la /data/ 2>&1; echo "---"; for f in /data/list1.txt /data/list2.txt /data/list3.txt; do echo "== $f =="; cat "$f" 2>&1; echo; done

cat /data/list1.txt /data/list2.txt /data/list3.txt \
  | sed 's/[[:space:]]*$//' \
  | grep -v '^$' \
  | sort -u > /data/combined.txt

echo "== combined.txt =="
cat /data/combined.txt
echo "---"
echo "Total unique emails: $(wc -l < /data/combined.txt)"

tool_outputs.commands: ls -la /data/ 2>&1; echo "---"; for f in /data/list1.txt /data/list2.txt /data/list3.txt; do echo "== $f =="; cat "$f" 2>&1; echo; done

  | sort -u > /data/combined.txt
